leadText: Palpitoad (Japanese: ガマガル Gamagaru) is a dual-type Water/Ground Pokémon introduced in Generation V. It evolves from Tympole starting at level 25 and evolves into Seismitoad starting at level 36.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Palpitoad sticking out its long tongue Palpitoad is an amphibious, bipedal Pokémon with an ovoid body. It is mostly blue, with a light tan oval marking lined with black stretching from the bottom of its belly to its forehead. Its black eyes are round. Its upper lip is round as well and curves down sharply at the corners. Inside its mouth is a long, sticky tongue used to immobilize and capture prey. Its feet are small with three toes each, and its tail is oval and white. Three large half-spheres encircle its head, each one light turquoise with a black base. There are four round, light blue bumps starting on either side of its lower body and stretching across its back. The bumps on its head can be vibrated to create waves and earthquakes, as well as intense sounds to weaken its prey. The larger a Palpitoad's lumps are, the wider the range of sounds it can make. Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Palpitoad evolves from Tympole and evolves into Seismitoad . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Tympole Water → First Evolution Palpitoad Water Ground → Second Evolution Seismitoad Water Ground

sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry BW031 Palpitoad Ash's Pokédex Palpitoad, the Vibration Pokémon. Palpitoad can live both underwater and on dry land. It captures prey by using its long sticky tongue. BW032 Palpitoad Ash's Pokédex Palpitoad, the Vibration Pokémon. Palpitoad's long, sticky tongue is used to capture prey. It lives both on land and in the water.
